subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 16
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, community, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is a mammal that lives in the ocean?
questions.explanation: Whales are mammals; they breathe air, give birth to live young, and feed their babies milk.
questions.hint: It is the largest animal to ever live on Earth.

questions.q: What is the capital city of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra is the capital city of Australia, chosen as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne.

questions.q: Which gas do plants release during the day to help us breathe?
questions.explanation: Plants take in carbon dioxide and release oxygen as a byproduct of photosynthesis.
questions.hint: It is the gas humans need to inhale.

questions.q: What is the name of the nearest star to Earth?
questions.explanation: The Sun is a star, and it is the closest one to our planet, Earth.
questions.objective: Understand facts about the solar system.

questions.q: Which food is the best source of calcium for strong bones?
questions.explanation: Dairy products like milk are rich in calcium, which is essential for bone health.
questions.hint: It is a white drink that comes from cows.

questions.q: Who is the community helper that treats sick or injured people?
questions.explanation: Doctors are trained to diagnose and treat illnesses and injuries in people.
questions.hint: They work in hospitals or clinics.

questions.q: What happens to water when it reaches 0 degrees Celsius?
questions.explanation: At 0 degrees Celsius, water turns from liquid to solid ice.

questions.q: Which continent is known for having many pyramids?
questions.explanation: Egypt, located in Africa, is famous for its ancient pyramids.

questions.q: What is the force that prevents us from floating away into space?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the attractive force between all masses, and Earth's gravity keeps us grounded.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy habit to prevent germs?
questions.explanation: Regular handwashing is one of the most effective ways to prevent the spread of germs.
